document: RBI Master Direction on Information Technology Framework for NBFC Sector (2017) (India - Row 15)

sections.heading: As you are aware, the Income Declaration Scheme, 2016 (the Scheme)   has
sections.summary: Income Declaration Scheme 2016 effective from June 1, 2016; banks hesitant to accept large cash deposits for government accounts.
sections.source_quote: 2. As you are aware, the Income Declaration Scheme, 2016 (the Scheme) has come into effect from June 1, 2016 and some declarants may like to pay their tax dues in cash.

sections.heading: We advise that banks must invariably accept cash, irrespective of amount, over
sections.summary: Banks must accept cash deposits of any amount from declarants, including under Income Declaration Scheme, with KYC compliance.

requirements.text: banks must invariably accept cash, irrespective of amount, over the counters from all declarants who desire to deposit cash at the counters, including deposits under the above Scheme through challan ITNS- 286
banks shall comply with the Know Your Customer requirements for customers and walk-in customers

sections.source_quote: 3. We advise that banks must invariably accept cash, irrespective of amount, over the counters from all declarants who desire to deposit cash at the counters, including deposits under the above Scheme through challan ITNS- 286.
